componentDescription: Upload provides a file selection area with drag-and-drop support. Use for profile photo uploads, document attachments, CSV imports, and any scenario requiring file input. Displays upload area with accept type hints and size constraints.

usageExamples.title: Profile photo upload
usageExamples.description: Single image upload for user profile picture.
usageExamples.code:
<Upload
  label="Profile photo"
  hint="JPG or PNG, max 5MB"
  accept="image/jpeg,image/png"
  maxSize={5 * 1024 * 1024}
  onUpload={(files) => handleProfilePhotoUpload(files[0])}
/>

usageExamples.title: Multi-file document upload
usageExamples.description: Upload area accepting multiple PDF documents.
usageExamples.code:
<Upload
  label="Supporting documents"
  hint="Upload up to 10 PDF files, max 10MB each"
  accept="application/pdf"
  multiple
  maxSize={10 * 1024 * 1024}
  onUpload={handleDocumentUpload}
/>

usageExamples.title: CSV data import
usageExamples.code:
<Upload
  label="Import data"
  hint="Upload a CSV file with the required column headers"
  accept=".csv,text/csv"
  onUpload={(files) => handleCsvImport(files[0])}
/>

onUpload.llmContext: Required. Callback receiving a FileList or File[] when the user selects or drops files. Process the files here — upload to a server, read contents, or store in state. Always validate file type and size before uploading even if accept and maxSize are set.
onUpload.propDescription: Required. Called with selected/dropped File array

accept.llmContext: MIME type or file extension filter string (e.g., 'image/jpeg,image/png' or '.pdf,.docx'). This restricts the file browser and provides a hint to users. Use specific MIME types for precision. Always validate on the server side as well.
accept.propDescription: Accepted file MIME types or extensions

multiple.llmContext: Set to true to allow selecting multiple files at once. Leave false (default) for single file fields like profile photos. When multiple is true, onUpload receives an array of all selected files.

maxSize.llmContext: Maximum file size in bytes. The component shows an error if the selected file exceeds this. Common values: 1MB = 1048576, 5MB = 5242880, 10MB = 10485760. Always display the limit in the hint text so users know before attempting upload.

disabled.llmContext: Set to true to prevent file selection. Use when the upload feature is gated (e.g., on a free plan) or when the form is in a read-only/submitted state.
